tagline: Yesterday's good news, bright and early.

stories.headline: Afghan refugee women cricketers find their voice on the field in England
stories.summary: Afghan refugee women, including journalist Zahra Joya, play historic cricket matches at Cambridge's Fenner's ground, inspiring girls back home under Taliban rule.

stories.headline: Firefighter finishes stranger's lawn so her family can focus on recovery
stories.summary: Lt. Colton Brown mows the yard to lift one worry from a family in crisis.

stories.headline: Tiny Cape Verde makes World Cup history on their fearless debut
stories.summary: Cape Verde's 525,000 people become the smallest nation ever to reach World Cup knockout stages on their tournament debut, with 40-year-old goalkeeper Vozinha the hero.

stories.headline: Breakthrough drug restores light to people with progressive blindness
stories.summary: First human trial of a photoswitch drug gives people with retinitis pigmentosa back their light perception—a milestone in vision restoration.

stories.headline: Cape Verde's coach celebrates his underdog dreamers after historic World Cup leap
stories.summary: Coach Bubista, 56, celebrates Cape Verde's fearless first World Cup campaign as they reach the knockout round without losing a single match.

stories.headline: Philippine tamaraw population grows 12%, calves thriving in protected forests
stories.summary: Indigenous communities celebrate as the world's rarest buffalo species adds 45 new individuals, with healthy calves born in the wild.

stories.headline: New Hampshire rallies to save the endangered Karner blue butterfly
stories.summary: About 400 New Hampshire neighbors donated to help purchase nearly seven acres of pine barrens, saving critical habitat for the rare Karner blue butterfly.

stories.headline: Blessing box restocked again and again as neighbors give generously
stories.summary: Ashley Vargas and 10-year-old Pepper Loden's blessing box in McGregor overflows so often with donations it needs constant restocking, with Pepper donating bracelets with words like 'hope' and 'smile.'

stories.headline: NASA drone successfully delivers human kidney, transforming transplant logistics
stories.summary: A NASA drone successfully flew a kidney beyond line of sight on June 5, marking the first step toward speeding organ delivery for transplant patients.

stories.headline: Critically endangered pangolin and civet returned safely to the wild
stories.summary: Meghalaya's team successfully released a critically endangered Chinese Pangolin and injured Asian Palm Civet into Nongkhyllem Wildlife Sanctuary after rehabilitation and recovery.

stories.headline: Bus driver receives keys to her Habitat for Humanity home
stories.summary: A bus driver's dream home becomes real through community partnership, a reward for hard work and hope.

stories.headline: Elusive bird finally spotted after a decade of patient searching
stories.summary: Researchers Rajkishore Swain and Subhani Rath's decade-long search finally confirmed the Rufous-fronted Babbler in Eastern India, establishing a new population for this elusive species.

stories.headline: Flexible AI skin patch becomes your instant personal doctor
stories.summary: University of Chicago researchers create a wearable patch that delivers medical insights and heart monitoring directly on the body.

stories.headline: Wet coffee grounds transformed into clean fuel in 90 seconds
stories.summary: South Korean researchers turn discarded coffee waste into high-quality biochar, solving waste while creating renewable energy.

stories.headline: Luka Modrić makes history as 40-year-old Croatia stages dramatic comeback
stories.summary: At 40, Luka Modrić sets a World Cup assist record as Croatia stuns Ghana 2-1 in a thrilling group-stage comeback.
